_metadata.source: Estimated from RateMyProfessors difficulty ratings, crowd-sourced student reviews, and course catalog analysis (April 2026). Bryant University does not publish official grade distributions. These are qualitative estimates, not official data.
_metadata.methodology: RateMyProfessors difficulty ratings (1-5 scale) were collected for Bryant Finance professors and cross-referenced with student comments about typical grades. Courses from the 'Easiest Classes at Bryant' article (humansofuniversity.com) were assigned lower difficulty. Estimates use the following heuristic: difficulty 1.0-2.0 = easy (avg GPA ~3.5+), 2.0-3.0 = moderate (avg GPA ~3.0-3.4), 3.0-4.0 = hard (avg GPA ~2.5-3.0), 4.0-5.0 = very hard (avg GPA ~2.0-2.5).

FIN 201.notes: Foundational finance course. Professor-dependent difficulty. Kuang (1.9 difficulty, easy A), Kumar (2.9), Fellingham (3.2), Koplik (3.5), Derderian (3.2), Zheng (3.3), Ramirez (3.6). Most students report B+ to A- range with moderate effort.

FIN 310.notes: Intermediate Corporate Finance. Builds heavily on FIN 201. Excel-based analysis. Students report challenging exams with capital budgeting and valuation problems. Fewer professor options than FIN 201.

FIN 312.notes: Investments. Uses Financial Market Center. Covers risk/return, CAPM, portfolio theory. Fellingham teaches this (3.2 difficulty). Students report B to B+ typical with study effort.

FIN 315.notes: Financial Institutions and Markets. Koplik teaches this frequently (3.5 difficulty). Conceptual course covering banking, insurance, capital markets. B range typical.

FIN 368.notes: Multinational Finance. Zheng teaches this (3.3 difficulty). Covers hedging, arbitrage, international capital budgeting.

FIN 370.notes: Financial Statement Analysis. Zbib teaches this (2.8 difficulty). Semester-long portfolio project. Student review: 'easy A if you put in effort.' Fellingham also teaches (3.2 difficulty).

FIN 380.notes: Financial Modeling. Ramirez teaches this and students rate it 5.0 difficulty. Heavy Excel/spreadsheet work. Students report C+ to B- typical. One of the hardest FIN courses.

FIN 381.notes: Risk Management and Insurance. Derderian teaches (3.2 difficulty). Part of Financial Services concentration.

FIN 384.notes: Credit Essentials. Commercial banking focus. Limited RMP data available.

FIN 412.notes: Introduction to Fintech and Digital Innovation. Newer course. Limited student feedback available.

FIN 414.notes: In sections.json catalog. Limited RMP data available.

FIN 458.notes: Debt Securities, Derivatives and Investing. Quantitative course covering options pricing, fixed income. 400-level, advanced content.

FIN 460.notes: Corporate Finance: Theory and Practice. Capstone course. Kumar teaches (2.9 difficulty for FIN 460). Case study heavy. A- reported by at least one student.

FIN 465.notes: Innovations in Contemporary Finance. Financial engineering focus. 400-level quantitative course.

FIN 466.notes: Data Analysis for Finance. Big data tools, open-source analytics. Requires MATH 201 + ACG 203 + FIN 201.

FIN 475.notes: Management of Banking Institutions. Asset/liability management strategies.

ACG 203.notes: Principles of Financial Accounting. Business core requirement. Accounting courses at Bryant are generally considered rigorous.

ACG 204.notes: Principles of Managerial Accounting. Business core. Builds on ACG 203.

BUS 400.notes: Business Policy. Senior capstone. Group project heavy.

ISA 201.notes: Introduction to Information Technology and Analytics. Business core. Generally accessible.

LGLS 211.notes: Legal Environment of Business. Business core. Reading-heavy but fair grading.

MKT 201.notes: Foundations of Marketing Management. Business core. Generally considered one of the more accessible business core courses.

MGT 200.notes: Management Principles and Practice. Business core. Group projects common.

MGT 201.notes: Operations Management. Business core. Quantitative elements with supply chain focus.

COM 203.notes: Introduction to Communication. Listed as one of the easiest courses at Bryant. Montecalvo teaches with 100% would-take-again and 1.7 difficulty.

COM 230.notes: Communication course option for LCS requirement.

PSY 260.notes: Introduction to Psychology. Listed as one of the easiest courses at Bryant (humansofuniversity.com).

POLS 241.notes: Introduction to Global Politics. Listed as one of the easiest courses at Bryant.

GEN 390.notes: General Education Capstone. Senior-level gen ed course.

LCS 270.notes: Introduction to Cultural Studies. Listed as one of the easiest courses at Bryant (humansofuniversity.com).

SCI 251.notes: Biology I Principles of Biology. Listed as one of the easiest courses at Bryant. Lab component (SCI L251) paired.

SCI 351.notes: Upper-level science course. 300-level courses generally harder.

ECO 113.notes: Microeconomic Principles. Introductory economics. Liam Rice (100% would-take-again, 2.7 difficulty). Sam Mirmirani (62% would-take-again, 3.0 difficulty).

MATH 201.notes: Statistics I. Required for many business courses. Quantitative but introductory level.

_professor_ratings._note: RateMyProfessors data for Bryant University Finance department professors, collected April 2026

Huan Kuang.student_notes: Easy A. All exams and quizzes are take home. Curves exams and allows retakes.

Jeffrey Cerutti.student_notes: Highest rated finance professor alongside Kuang.

Poojan Patel.student_notes: Very easy, highly rated but limited sample size.

John Fellingham.student_notes: Accessible and student-friendly. Tests are Lockdown Browser plus take-home. A and B+ grades reported.

Mara Derderian.student_notes: Grade depends on exams, which are tough. B+ to C- range common. Attendance critical.

Cathy Zheng.student_notes: Knowledgeable but lectures can be dry. A to B grade range. Exams difficult but similar to homework.

Sonal Kumar.student_notes: Wants students to succeed. Open note exams and quizzes. Lenient on due dates.

Leila Zbib.student_notes: Easy A if you put in effort. Semester-long portfolio project can be time-consuming.

Andres Ramirez.student_notes: FIN 380 rated 5.0 difficulty. C+ to B+ grades typical. Many students recommend avoiding.

Jeffrey Koplik.student_notes: Knowledgeable but demanding. Hard exams. Success requires preparation and interest in finance.

_finance_concentration_requirements._note: Official Bryant University Finance Concentration requirements from catalog.bryant.edu (2025-2026 catalog)
_finance_concentration_requirements.concentration_name: Bachelor of Science in Business Administration: Finance Concentration

courses.name: Archway Financial Statement and Security Analysis

elective_group_1.notes: FIN 370 and FIN 371 are equivalent courses. Cannot get credit for both.

elective_group_2.description: Two additional Finance electives (must include at least one 400-level course)

eligible_courses.name: Introduction to Fintech and Digital Innovation

business_core_requirements.name: Introduction to Information Technology and Analytics

prerequisite_chain._note: The critical prerequisite chain for Finance concentration courses
chain: FIN 201 + FIN 312 + ACG 203 --> FIN 371 (also needs Junior standing + Instructor approval)
FIN 201 + FIN 310 + FIN 312 --> FIN 460 (also needs Junior standing)
ACG 203 + FIN 201 + MATH 201 --> FIN 466 (also needs Junior standing)

_crowd_sourced_difficulty._note: Qualitative difficulty assessments compiled from humansofuniversity.com, Niche, RateMyProfessors, and student reviews
easiest_courses: COM 203 - Introduction to Communication (Montecalvo: 1.7 difficulty, 100% would-take-again)
COM 251 - Written Communication (listed in easiest classes article)
PSY 260 - Introduction to Psychology (listed in easiest classes article)
LCS 270 - Introduction to Cultural Studies (listed in easiest classes article)
LCS 230 - Introduction to Film Studies (listed in easiest classes article)
SCI 251 - Biology I (listed in easiest classes article)
LCS 121 - Introduction to Literary Studies (listed in easiest classes article)
WRIT 106 / GEN 106 - Writing Workshop (listed in easiest classes article)
POLS 241 - Introduction to Global Politics (listed in easiest classes article)
AS 391 - General Arts and Sciences Internship (listed in easiest classes article)
hardest_finance_courses: FIN 458 - Debt Securities, Derivatives and Investing (quantitative, 400-level)
FIN 460 - Corporate Finance: Theory and Practice (capstone, case study heavy)
FIN 310 - Intermediate Corporate Finance (builds heavily on FIN 201)
FIN 465 - Innovations in Contemporary Finance (financial engineering)
general_observations: Bryant is described as 'intellectually challenging yet informative' - not an easy school
Professor selection matters enormously: same course (FIN 201) ranges from 1.9 to 3.6 difficulty
Finance concentration is considered one of the more rigorous business majors at Bryant
Gen ed and humanities courses are generally easier GPA boosters
Average admitted student GPA is 3.53 (weighted), suggesting grade inflation is moderate
